Fitaviz 1 Overview We derive steps from published methods to process, visualize and extract activity summary statistics from Fitbit data. The focus is on adequately capturing activity patterns that ultimately support epidemiological findings. 1.1 Methods Extensive use of accelerometers in health research has led to some level of agreement of which issues processing of epoch accelerometer values should address, most notably how to determine non-wear time and what constitutes a valid or adherent day. We collect processing decisions from the literature, mostly approaches widely adopted for ActiGraph’s activity counts, and illustrate their application on Fitbit’s steps and HR minute data. Wear-time non-wear detection with activity counts is typically based on the length of periods of low acceleration. As for FitBit, minutes with non-empty HR and non-empty steps are labeled as wear-time. Wear minutes between 8am and 8pm are summed across each day to obtain daytime wear. Adherent day to account for potential wear-time diurnal bias, adherent days are days with at least 10 hours of daytime wear. Adherent time refers to wear minutes within an adherent day. Fig. 1 Depiction of daytime wear. For each day, wear minutes are aggregated according to their physical activity classification. Fitbit’s proprietary algorithm classifies each minute in four activity-levels: sedentary, light, moderate and vigorous. The first panel shows daily total counts in adherent and non-adherent days, the second panel shows the derived proportion of time spent in each activity-level. Proportion of time spent in each activity-level are representative of diurnal activity only on adherent days, and are likely misrepresentations on non-adherent days.

2 Summaries Data Dictionary patients_summary.csv - patient level entries daily_data.csv - patient-day level entries daily_summary.csv - patient level entries 2.1 patients_summary.csv Summaries describing patient’s wear and activity from min_date to max_date. Following the steps described in Methods, daytime wear minutes are labeled and classified as adherent time or non-adherent time. Column names make the distiction between summary statistics that take adherent wear time AWT or that take non-adherent wear NAW. Representative daily summaries of activity .
